pi_items.source: Task description (line 5): 'Create a target channel named Unstable_Service'; setup_task.sh; export_result.sh line 12
Task description (line 5): 'Create a watchdog channel named System_Watchdog'; export_result.sh line 13
Task description (line 5): 'Append a log entry to /home/ga/watchdog.log'; setup_task.sh line 25; export_result.sh line 89

Task description (line 5): 'runs every 10 seconds' (10 seconds = 10000 milliseconds); export_result.sh line 65 confirms 10s poll interval; Mirth Connect documentation confirms polling frequency is in milliseconds

privileged_info_summary: This task requires implementing a self-healing watchdog system in Mirth Connect (NextGen Connect v4.5.0, a real healthcare integration engine). The agent must create two channels: 'Unstable_Service' (TCP Listener on port 7000) and 'System_Watchdog' (JavaScript Reader polling every 10 seconds). The watchdog must use the real ChannelUtil API to detect when Unstable_Service stops, automatically restart it, and log events to /home/ga/watchdog.log. The task data is synthetic (created during agent execution) but the software is real. All metadata configuration parameters are correctly specified.
